title: lupinus polyphyllus var. prunophilus
intro: Lupinus polyphyllus var. prunophilus, also known as the Buttercup Lupine, is a perennial legume native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant contains alkaloids, including lupanine, which have been found to possess medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Buttercup Lupine include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and congestion, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Lupinus polyphyllus var. prunophilus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support the body's natural healing processes.
